column2: A user says one application prints incorrectly. What should you print first to help isolate the problem?
column3: Use output that does not depend on the user's application.
column4: Print a printer or operating-system test page.
column5:
Why

A test page helps separate an application or document problem from the printer, driver, and print path. If the test page prints normally, the printer hardware is less likely to be the only issue.
column9: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.56; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 5.6
column10: A+::220-1201::5.6 A+::220-1201::Domain5-Troubleshooting A+::220-1201::TroubleshootingPrinters Basic HighYield Printer Scenario TestPage Source::Messer-v170

column2: A network printer has a built-in web utility. What is the support value of using it during troubleshooting?
column4: It can provide printer-side diagnostic information without relying only on the user's application.
column5:
Why

Printer diagnostics can show device state, configuration, or errors from the printer's perspective. The exact utility varies by vendor, so the card tests the decision to use diagnostics, not a model-specific menu path.
column9: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.56; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 5.6
column10: A+::220-1201::5.6 A+::220-1201::Domain5-Troubleshooting A+::220-1201::TroubleshootingPrinters Basic Diagnostics Printer Source::Messer-v170

column2: An inkjet printer produces lines down the printed page. What maintenance action should you try?
column4: Clean the print heads.
column5:
Why

For inkjet printers, line defects commonly point to the print head or ink flow. Cleaning the print heads is the relevant first maintenance action before assuming the whole printer needs replacement.
column9: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.56; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 5.6
column10: A+::220-1201::5.6 A+::220-1201::Domain5-Troubleshooting A+::220-1201::TroubleshootingPrinters Basic HighYield Inkjet OutputQuality Printer Source::Messer-v170

column2: A laser printer produces a consistent line down the printed page. What printer component should you suspect?
column4: A scratched or damaged photosensitive drum.
column5:
Why

Laser printers use a drum in the imaging process. A repeated line defect on laser output is a clue to inspect or escalate the drum path rather than using inkjet maintenance logic.
column9: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.56; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 5.6
column10: A+::220-1201::5.6 A+::220-1201::Domain5-Troubleshooting A+::220-1201::TroubleshootingPrinters Basic HighYield Laser OutputQuality Printer Source::Messer-v170

column2: A printer produces faded prints or blank pages. What simple condition should you check first?
column4: Low toner or low ink.
column5:
Why

Faded output and blank pages often start with consumables. Checking toner or ink is faster and less invasive than replacing printer hardware or changing drivers.
column9: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.56; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 5.6
column10: A+::220-1201::5.6 A+::220-1201::Domain5-Troubleshooting A+::220-1201::TroubleshootingPrinters Basic HighYield Consumables OutputQuality Printer Source::Messer-v170

column2: A laser printer leaves a faint repeated copy of earlier page content on the same sheet. What does that symptom suggest?
column4: The drum is not being cleaned properly during the laser printing process.
column5:
Why

Ghost or echo images are repeated remnants from earlier drum rotation. The important troubleshooting target is the laser imaging or cleaning process, not an application formatting issue.
column9: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.56; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 5.6
column10: A+::220-1201::5.6 A+::220-1201::Domain5-Troubleshooting A+::220-1201::TroubleshootingPrinters Basic Ghosting Laser Printer Source::Messer-v170

column2: A printer outputs unreadable symbols instead of the expected document. What is a likely software-side cause?
column4: A bad or incorrect printer driver, including the wrong printer model or page description language.
column5:
Why

Garbled output can happen when the printer receives data it does not interpret correctly. Driver and page-description-language checks are more targeted than assuming the paper path or toner is at fault.
column9: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.56; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 5.6
column10: A+::220-1201::5.6 A+::220-1201::Domain5-Troubleshooting A+::220-1201::TroubleshootingPrinters Basic HighYield Driver GarbledPrint Printer Source::Messer-v170

column2: A printer test page is normal, but one application still prints incorrectly. What does that result suggest?
column4: Focus on the application, document output, or driver path rather than the printer engine alone.
column5:
Why

A normal test page proves the printer can produce clean output outside the application. The next theory should be closer to the application or driver interaction that creates the bad job.
column9: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.56; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 5.6
column10: A+::220-1201::5.6 A+::220-1201::Domain5-Troubleshooting A+::220-1201::TroubleshootingPrinters Basic Application Printer TestPage Source::Messer-v170

column2: When clearing a printer paper jam, what should you avoid doing?
column3: Protect the printer mechanism while removing the paper.
column4: Avoid tearing the paper or damaging internal printer components.
column5:
Why

Forced removal can leave scraps inside the paper path or damage rollers and sensors. Careful removal preserves the evidence and prevents a simple jam from becoming a hardware repair.
column9: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.56; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 5.6
column10: A+::220-1201::5.6 A+::220-1201::Domain5-Troubleshooting A+::220-1201::TroubleshootingPrinters Basic HighYield PaperJam Printer Safety Source::Messer-v170

column2: Paper is stuck deep inside a printer and does not come out easily. What is the safest next step?
column4: Stop forcing it and follow the printer's approved jam-removal process or escalate for service.
column5:
Why

Printer paper paths vary by model. If paper does not release normally, forcing it can damage internal components or leave torn pieces behind.
column9: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.56; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 5.6
column10: A+::220-1201::5.6 A+::220-1201::Domain5-Troubleshooting A+::220-1201::TroubleshootingPrinters Basic PaperJam Printer Safety Source::Messer-v170

column2: A printer does not feed paper or pulls multiple sheets at once. What should you check first?
column4: Check the paper tray and paper loading.
column5:
Why

Misfeeds can come from the tray, paper stack, or paper placement. Checking the tray is a simple first step before assuming the printer needs major service.
column9: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.56; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 5.6
column10: A+::220-1201::5.6 A+::220-1201::Domain5-Troubleshooting A+::220-1201::TroubleshootingPrinters Basic PaperFeed Printer Tray Source::Messer-v170

column2: A laser printer repeatedly fails to pick up paper even when the tray is loaded correctly. What maintenance item may be involved?
column4: Pickup rollers, which are commonly handled as part of laser printer maintenance.
column5:
Why

When tray loading is correct but feed problems repeat, the pickup mechanism becomes a stronger theory. The card tests the maintenance clue, not a model-specific roller replacement process.
column9: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.56; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 5.6
column10: A+::220-1201::5.6 A+::220-1201::Domain5-Troubleshooting A+::220-1201::TroubleshootingPrinters Basic Laser PaperFeed Printer Source::Messer-v170

column2: Printed pages come out creased. What should the technician check?
column4: Check the paper path and verify the paper weight is appropriate.
column5:
Why

Creases point to a physical handling problem as paper moves through the printer. The paper itself can also be part of the issue if its weight does not match what the printer expects.
column9: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.56; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 5.6
column10: A+::220-1201::5.6 A+::220-1201::Domain5-Troubleshooting A+::220-1201::TroubleshootingPrinters Basic PaperPath Printer Source::Messer-v170

column2: Many jobs are pending for the same printer. What should you inspect first?
column3: Look where jobs wait before they print.
column4: Inspect the print queue for a stuck or corrupt job.
column5:
Why

One bad job can block later jobs and make the printer look offline or broken. Queue inspection is a low-risk first check before repeatedly sending new jobs or replacing hardware.
column9: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.56; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 5.6
column10: A+::220-1201::5.6 A+::220-1201::Domain5-Troubleshooting A+::220-1201::TroubleshootingPrinters Basic HighYield PrintQueue Printer Spooler Source::Messer-v170

column2: A Windows print spooler repeatedly crashes. What evidence source can help confirm the print-service problem?
column4: Windows print-service logs, such as Event Viewer or Windows-PrintService entries.
column5:
Why

Repeated spooler failures should be documented with logs when available. Logs help show whether a service, job, driver, or queue issue is recurring instead of relying only on user reports.
column9: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.56; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 5.6
column10: A+::220-1201::5.6 A+::220-1201::Domain5-Troubleshooting A+::220-1201::TroubleshootingPrinters Basic Logs Printer Spooler Source::Messer-v170

column2: After one specific print job is removed, the remaining queued jobs print normally. What did that result identify?
column4: That job was likely the blocking or corrupt job in the queue.
column5:
Why

The result isolates the queue problem to one job rather than the printer as a whole. Document the job, user, and file or application involved before closing or escalating the ticket.
column9: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.56; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 5.6
column10: A+::220-1201::5.6 A+::220-1201::Domain5-Troubleshooting A+::220-1201::TroubleshootingPrinters Basic PrintQueue Printer Scenario Source::Messer-v170

column2: A printer starts making grinding noises. What category of problem should you suspect first?
column4: A mechanical problem, such as a jammed paper path or stalled carriage.
column5:
Why

Grinding is not a normal output-quality symptom. It points to something moving incorrectly inside the printer, so the technician should avoid sending more jobs until the mechanical path is checked.
column9: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.56; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 5.6
column10: A+::220-1201::5.6 A+::220-1201::Domain5-Troubleshooting A+::220-1201::TroubleshootingPrinters Basic HighYield Mechanical Printer Scenario Source::Messer-v170

column2: A printer has a jam and the manual describes a specific clearing sequence. What should the technician follow?
column4: Follow the printer's documented jam-removal procedure for that model.
column5:
Why

Printers have different paper paths and access panels. The objective supports using the approved process; it does not require memorizing model-specific disassembly steps.
column9: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.56; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 5.6
column10: A+::220-1201::5.6 A+::220-1201::Domain5-Troubleshooting A+::220-1201::TroubleshootingPrinters Basic Mechanical Printer Safety Source::Messer-v170

column2: Grinding continues after visible paper is cleared from the printer. What is an appropriate next decision?
column4: Escalate for maintenance or replacement according to support policy.
column5:
Why

Persistent grinding suggests a mechanical issue beyond a simple visible jam. Continuing to run jobs can cause more damage, so the technician should document the symptom and escalate.
column9: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.56; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 5.6
column10: A+::220-1201::5.6 A+::220-1201::Domain5-Troubleshooting A+::220-1201::TroubleshootingPrinters Basic Escalation Mechanical Printer Source::Messer-v170

column2: In printer troubleshooting, when do finishing problems occur in the print process?
column4: After the printer has applied ink or toner to the page.
column5:
Why

Finishing includes post-print handling such as collating, stapling, binding, or hole punching. That means the image may print correctly while the finishing step fails.
column9: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.57; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 5.6
column10: A+::220-1201::5.6 A+::220-1201::Domain5-Troubleshooting A+::220-1201::TroubleshootingPrinters Basic Finishing Printer Source::Messer-v170

column2: A large office printer has a staple jam. What should the technician avoid?
column4: Avoid forcing the finisher open or clearing it without the approved removal process.
column5:
Why

Finishing units differ by manufacturer. Following the approved process reduces the risk of damaging the stapling or collating mechanism.
column9: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.57; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 5.6
column10: A+::220-1201::5.6 A+::220-1201::Domain5-Troubleshooting A+::220-1201::TroubleshootingPrinters Basic Finishing Printer Safety Source::Messer-v170

column2: Printed documents are hole-punched in the wrong location. What should you verify first?
column4: Verify the printer driver settings for the finishing option.
column5:
Why

Hole-punch placement is tied to finishing configuration. Driver settings are a focused first check before assuming the physical finisher is broken.
column9: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.57; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 5.6
column10: A+::220-1201::5.6 A+::220-1201::Domain5-Troubleshooting A+::220-1201::TroubleshootingPrinters Basic Driver Finishing Printer Source::Messer-v170

column2: A user prints a portrait document, but it comes out landscape. What should you check first?
column4: Check the print settings and printer driver orientation settings.
column5:
Why

Orientation is commonly controlled in the print dialog and driver. Checking those settings is faster and safer than replacing printer hardware.
column9: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.57; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 5.6
column10: A+::220-1201::5.6 A+::220-1201::Domain5-Troubleshooting A+::220-1201::TroubleshootingPrinters Basic HighYield Orientation Printer Source::Messer-v170

column2: Orientation is wrong for jobs from multiple users even after they choose the correct orientation while printing. What setting should be checked next?
column4: Check the printer's default orientation setting at the printer console or device configuration.
column5:
Why

If several users see the same orientation behavior, the printer's default setting becomes a stronger theory than one user's print dialog.
column9: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.57; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 5.6
column10: A+::220-1201::5.6 A+::220-1201::Domain5-Troubleshooting A+::220-1201::TroubleshootingPrinters Basic Orientation Printer Source::Messer-v170

column2: An office printer has multiple trays, but a user's computer does not show one of them. What should you compare?
column4: Compare the user's printer driver configuration with the trays actually installed in the printer.
column5:
Why

Tray definitions are part of printer configuration. The tray may physically exist, but the user's driver may not know it is installed or what paper it contains.
column9: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.57; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 5.6
column10: A+::220-1201::5.6 A+::220-1201::Domain5-Troubleshooting A+::220-1201::TroubleshootingPrinters Basic HighYield Driver Printer Tray Source::Messer-v170

column2: A legal-size document is sent to a tray loaded with letter-size paper. What configuration mismatch should you identify?
column4: The job's page size does not match the paper size in the selected tray.
column5:
Why

Tray selection must match the requested page size and loaded paper. This card tests the size mismatch, not a deep printer repair process.
column9: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.57; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 5.6
column10: A+::220-1201::5.6 A+::220-1201::Domain5-Troubleshooting A+::220-1201::TroubleshootingPrinters Basic PaperSize Printer Tray Source::Messer-v170

column2: A printer is unavailable on the network. What should you confirm before changing IP settings?
column3: Start by identifying how the device is supposed to connect.
column4: Confirm the printer's connection type, such as wired, wireless, or another configured path.
column5:
Why

Printer connectivity follows the same basic path-isolation logic as other network devices. You need to know the intended connection before deciding which network settings or links to test.
column9: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.57; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 5.6
column10: A+::220-1201::5.6 A+::220-1201::Domain5-Troubleshooting A+::220-1201::TroubleshootingPrinters Basic HighYield Connectivity Printer Scenario Source::Messer-v170

column2: A network printer is connected, but users cannot print to it. What IP configuration should you verify?
column4: Verify the printer's IP address, subnet mask, default gateway, and DNS settings where applicable.
column5:
Why

A network printer is still a network device. Incorrect addressing or gateway information can prevent clients or print servers from reaching it.
column9: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.57; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 5.6
column10: A+::220-1201::5.6 A+::220-1201::Domain5-Troubleshooting A+::220-1201::TroubleshootingPrinters Basic Connectivity Network Printer Source::Messer-v170

column2: Jobs leave the user's computer but never reach the printer, and the environment uses a shared print server. What should you check?
column4: Check the print server or print server queue in the path.
column5:
Why

The print server can be part of the printer path. If jobs leave the client but do not arrive at the printer, the server or shared queue is a logical boundary to test.
column9: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.57; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 5.6
column10: A+::220-1201::5.6 A+::220-1201::Domain5-Troubleshooting A+::220-1201::TroubleshootingPrinters Basic PrintServer Printer Scenario Source::Messer-v170

column2: Users can open the printer's web interface, but print jobs remain stuck on a server queue. What does that result suggest?
column4: The printer is reachable, so focus next on the print server, queue, or job path.
column5:
Why

Reaching the printer interface makes a total network outage less likely. The remaining symptom points back toward the queue or server layer that handles the jobs.
column9: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.57; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 5.6
column10: A+::220-1201::5.6 A+::220-1201::Domain5-Troubleshooting A+::220-1201::TroubleshootingPrinters Basic Connectivity Printer Scenario Source::Messer-v170

column2: You are escalating a repeated bad-output issue. What evidence should you include?
column4: Test-page result, printer type, visible defect, affected users or applications, consumable status, and recent maintenance.
column5:
Why

Escalation is more useful when it separates printer output from application output and describes the exact defect. This prevents the next technician from repeating the same isolation steps.
column9: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.56; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 5.6
column10: A+::220-1201::5.6 A+::220-1201::Domain5-Troubleshooting A+::220-1201::TroubleshootingPrinters Basic Documentation OutputQuality Printer Source::Messer-v170

column2: You are escalating a shared print queue or printer connectivity issue. What should you document?
column4: Affected scope, queue state, printer connection type, IP configuration, print server status, and any relevant log entries.
column5:
Why

Queue and connectivity problems often involve multiple layers. Clear documentation helps the next support tier see whether the issue is client-side, server-side, printer-side, or network-related.
column9: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.56; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 5.6
column10: A+::220-1201::5.6 A+::220-1201::Domain5-Troubleshooting A+::220-1201::TroubleshootingPrinters Basic Documentation PrintQueue Printer Source::Messer-v170
